description_long: This helmet features a rounded, full-coverage shell with a weathered, metallic gray finish and prominent black scuff marks and streaks, giving it a rugged, battle-worn appearance. The front is equipped with a dark, full-face visor that covers the eyes, and a series of vertical slits below the visor suggest a built-in ventilation or voice system. On each side, circular earpieces with grille-like details are present, possibly for communication or additional ventilation. The surface is marred with scratches, dirt, and red stains, enhancing its utilitarian, militaristic look. The helmet's design is reminiscent of science fiction or futuristic combat gear, with no visible external straps or fasteners. The overall construction appears solid and protective, suitable for urban tactical scenarios.
description: A weathered, metallic gray helmet with a full-face black visor, side earpieces, and battle-worn scuff marks. Its rugged, sci-fi design features ventilation slits and a solid, protective build.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the smooth, rounded back of the helmet with two rectangular vent-like details on either side and a marbled, worn surface.
description_view_1: The left-front angle highlights the side earpiece with grille details, the full-face visor, and prominent black and red scuff marks on the dome.
description_view_2: The front view displays the dark visor, vertical slits below it, and symmetrical cheek contours, with a central black streak and red stains on the upper shell.
description_view_3: The right-front angle mirrors the left, showing the right earpiece, visor, and additional surface wear, with the helmet's curvature and vent details visible.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Two rectangular vents on the rear sides and a series of vertical slits below the visor on the front; all vents are small and grille-like.
attribute_graphic_design: Weathered metallic gray with black streaks, red stains, and scuff marks; no distinct motifs or text, giving a distressed, battle-worn appearance.
